directive.title: RFQ Authorization Audit Log Schema & Migration

auditor.summary: The build successfully addresses all prior findings from the previous audit. The users table creation has been removed, TypeScript type exports are present, unused imports are cleaned up, and no test files with incompatible dependencies were generated. The authorization_audit_log table is defined with all required columns, CHECK constraints, and append-only enforcement via both trigger and RULEs. The only significant remaining issue is the missing foreign key constraint on user_id, which is a HIGH-severity issue because the build spec explicitly requires it. However, this is complicated by the fact that the users table does not exist in the ground truth schema, creating a spec contradiction. All acceptance criteria are either PASS or PARTIAL, and all prior CRITICAL and HIGH findings are resolved, so the build has converged per the convergence rule.

top_issues.description: Missing foreign key constraint on user_id column. The build spec explicitly requires 'user_id text NOT NULL referencing users.id (use .references(() => users.id))'. The current definition has user_id: text('user_id').notNull() without any FK reference. This means referential integrity is not enforced at the database level, which could allow orphaned records.

Missing foreign key constraint in migration. The migration creates the authorization_audit_log table without any FK constraint on user_id. The build spec requires referencing users.id with NO ACTION on delete.

The import of 'jsonb' from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core' is unused in the new authorizationAuditLog table definition. It was already imported for the existing auditLogs table, so this is not a new issue, but it's worth noting.
